feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Extended Warehouse Management
feed.subtitle: SAP Extended Warehouse Management Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: is IBD partial automatic packing possible?
entry.content:
Hello,

We have no ASN in this implementation so HU's will either be created automatically via packing specification and/or manually.

So, the scenario might be this one:

Several similar products are packed the same way: let's say the "outer box" contains 10 "inner boxes" of 10 units (outer box, 100un).

outer boxes are hu managed and stored on a hu managed storage type. inner boxes or "loose units" are not hu managed and stored on a non-hu managed storage type.

Skipping the discussion of order quantities, we might receive one inbound delivery as such:

- material#1, 103ea (1 outer box + 3 loose units)

- material#2, 108ea (1 outer box + 8 loose units)

- material#3, 140ea (1 outer box + 4 inner boxes)

the physical packing delivered by the supplier is:

- hu#1, material#1, 100ea --> moving to stg.type#1

- hu#2, material#2, 100ea --> moving to stg.type#1

- hu#3, material#3, 100ea --> moving to stg.type#1

- hu#4, material#1, 3ea + material#2, 8ea + material#3, 40ea --> moving to stg.type#2

so, when packing this IBD i would expect that automatic packing was able to partially pack each item, creating Hu#1, Hu#2, HU#3 and leave the remaining quantity unpacked; that unpacked quantity would be packed to hu#4 on receiving.

but automatic packing is on error, requiring to fully pack each item (and in the end, no packing at all) and i don't what might be the best solution to overcome this error.

is there a way to only partial pack what is covered by the packing specification?

should i leave a final level to pack all the remaining quantities?

is there a way to propose that final hu#4, mixing all loose quantities? (even so, since this is manually - eventually randomly - packed at the vendor, would we benefit anything by doing so?)

thank you for your insights,

best regards

entry.title: Inspection lot for Inspection document 17* does not exist (Message Class: QPLEXT)
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I'm configuring Quality Management in Decentral EWM, where I would like to give UD in QA32.

Once the GR is done in EWM, Inspection document in getting generated in EWM. But, Queue is getting stuck in ERP with Inspection lot for Inspection document 17* does not exist (Message Class: QPLEXT) this error.

I have verified the configs, but still not able to resolve it. I'm attaching the screenshot below of the Queue and its error.

I would really appreciate the help

entry.title: Compatibillity RF scanner SAP EWM
entry.content:
Dear community,

Our customer intends to use EWM (basic license) and has the following hardware:

  • Zebra MC3300X scanners

  • 4 WinMate truck terminals

Can this hardware be used with EWM, and are there any restrictions or specific conditions we should take into account?

Thanks in advance.

SAP Extended Warehouse Management SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

entry.title: SAP EWM - Pick, Pack, Stage - Destination of Pick-WT changed during confirmation
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

I setup POSC in order to perform Pick, Pack, Stage. I want to use rule based determination of Pack WC.

When I create the WT, WC is determined correctly according to Setup. Pick-HU is created successfully.

When I confirm the WT, the previously determined work center is replaced by the starting bin of the activity area - the place where the HU is created.

I tried with debugging and found a form routine in /SCWM/SAPLL03B "PICK_HU_MOVE_TO" - but here there is a check statement in place which exits the routine when a storage process is in place.

Later during processing the destination data is changed in "SRC_DEST_FILL" because TO Destination and HU-Location are not the same.

 

Do you have any idea where I might be missing something - my debugging skills come to an end.

Thx in advance and BR

Ben

entry.title: About EWM Note 2464460 (SAP-internal test purposes only)
entry.content:
In note 2464460 BC sets listed for embedded EWM are marked “SAP-internal test purposes only",
Does it indicates that these BC sets are not intended to be used as a standard, default configuration baseline for implementing a productive embedded EWM warehouse.?
if not then which BC sets should be considered to proceed implementing embedded EWM on S4HANA. 

Thank You
Kalyan

entry.title: What are standard FIori application can be use in SAP ECC WM system
entry.content:
What are standard FIori application can be use in SAP ECC Warehouse management syetsm and which fiori application can be use for Pickling and packing process. 

What are common application that can be used in WM and EWM ?

entry.title: Material Document button not visible in QA32 if the GR is done against HU in PRDI
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I'm doing GR against the Inbound delivery in PRDI using one HU at a time, when I do it inspection lot is getting generated against HU. But, when I open the inspection lot in QA32, material document button is not visible on top.

Whereas, If I do GR against the delivery as whole, using the Goods Receipt + Save button. Material Document is visible against the inspection lot in QA32.

It would be really helpful if someone can explain me why this happens and at the same time give some resolution such that I'm able to see the material document against the inspection lot even if I'm doing GR against the HU's.

Attaching the images for reference.

1. Below image is If I do GR against the IBD in PRDI.

2. This is how I performed the GR for inspection lot which has material document against it.

3. Below inspection lot is if I do GR against HU's in PRDI

4. This is how I performed the GR for inspection lot which has material document against it.


Regards,
Akhilesh Herkal

entry.title: Deleting entries from table /SCDL/DB_DF in EWM directly (EWM 9.5 SP8)
entry.content:
Hello all,

I've an requirement to delete entries from table /SCDL/DB_DF directly (i.e. w/ SE16), because it seems that they can't be corrected/adjusted w/ SAP program /SCWM/DLV_CHECKDATA.
Does anybody know, if this can cause inconsistencies or if it can be done w/o any risk?

I don't know  much about SAP EWM, but I found a note (3198881 - Document Flow Inconsistencies In EWM Delivery Orders (Inbound Or Outbound) ), which describes inconsistencies In EWM Delivery Orders, so I don't think it's a good idea to directly delete entries from that table.

Thanks & regards
Daniel

entry.title: EWM Component staging not initiated
entry.content:
Hello experts.

We are implementing EWM in public cloud. In our system we stage using material independent control cycle using pick part strategy as shown:

Now, what we do is, after release of production orders, we go to functions -> WM Material Staging -> Execute. When we do that we see that the documents generated in the process posting change item app as shown below:

So our customizing system is working fine. Now we are in test system and what we are noticing is when we execute the staging, the quantity is not requested as shown in the simulation below:

In the log I see the message: Message 118 - Batch verification no required, although possible in WM (pick parts):

We have never seen this message in customizing system. Also one more thing I have noticed that in the component view 'Change Process Type' field is marked as 1 (Manually Changed):

In customizing system I have seen this field is blank.

Could you please help me understand what is the root cause of this issue? Why am I not able to execute the staging process? Any help/insight will be highly appreciated.

 

Thank you

Snigdhodeep

entry.title: Outbound iDocs are not randomly not generated
entry.content:
Hi guys, 

For the consumption of a material during production, Warehouse task is created to transfer from Trolley to Floor(Bins). This triggers an iDoc from SAP to MES.

The issue we are facing is in last 6 months we have had 6 random incidents (different material number, batch number.......), at the same plant, where no outbound iDocs are generated destined for MES. So on SAP the material has been moved to Floor(bin). But on MES the material is still on Trolley(bin).

Contacted ABAP team and SAP for Support but we are not able to replicate this issue on demand. Since we cannot replicate the issue, the investigation is kind of stalled

Anyone faced this issue before? Anyone who can suggest where the issue might be?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Goods Issunce Against Production Order in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Hello,

My components storage location is EWM. My Production storage location is IM. I am doing goods issuance against outbound delivery created through MF60 staging of components w.r.t Production order.
When I post goods issue against outbound delivery, movement type 411 is performed automatically which shows stock at Production storage location. 
I have a demand. I want that when i post goods issue against Outbound delivery 313 movement type hits so that till production storage location guys doesn't confirm arrival of goods, it stays in transit.
I am using Public Cloud. Need assistance whether it is possible? If yes, guide me steps so that all time when goods issue is posted, 313 movement type hits.

entry.title: Picking a whole HU with mixed batches
entry.content:
We have a requirement to pick and pack HUs that we have in inventory. All of our products are batch managed. The picking and packing works well when the HUs have only one batch. Meaning, the source HU becomes the destination HU during WH task creation, as this is a HU WH Task. However there's a problem with HUs that have more than one batch because in this case the system creates a product WH task and as such the source HU is not set as the destination HU. And even during WH task confirmation we cannot enter the destination HU being the same as the source HU. If we do this we get error /SCWM/L3893 "Destination HU same as source HU only allowed for HU takeover". And if we try ticking the flag "HU Withdrawal" we get error message /SCWM/L3859 "Withdraw from HU XXXX only possible if total amount of HU is withdrawn". So either way we are stuck.

Does anybody experienced it before? In our case we must use the same handling unit number

entry.title: Type of store EWM without exit rules - pick point
entry.content:
Good afternoon, I share a concern that came to me, and I hope someone can help me with this: 
I request that for the type of warehouse AAAA, in warehouse AL01, the EWM configuration be changed so that it does not carry out withdrawal by FEFO. It must allow for the withdrawal of any batch. (This material is handled by HU and presents several batches).
This requirement arises because the product is stored in the type of warehouse AAAA in a chaotic way; when they are removed to the WIP (by means of the MF60), the operators remove the resin that they have most at hand. 
The idea is to ensure that the type of warehouse AAAA does not propose a batch when I create the warehouse task, but rather that when I perform picking in RF, I can **bleep** the batch that is closest.
It has been tested by removing the FEFO goods exit rules in SPRO for that type of warehouse: - Specify the search sequence for the type of warehouse
- Define warehouse exit control indicator
- Determine the warehouse type search sequence for output
Also, the output indicator from the master data in /scwm/mat1 was removed. With this it is achieved that the system does not let you create the task automatically but if by hand, but despite that it still proposes a particular batch.
It is impo

entry.title: Advanced Production Integration | Basic or Advanced EWM license?
entry.content:
Dear community,

Within SAP EWM, there are two approaches to production integration:

  1. Delivery-Based Production Integration

  2. Advanced Production Integration (based on Production Material Requests – PMRs)

We would like to clarify the licensing requirements for these two options.

Do both approaches fall under the basic EWM license, or is the advanced EWM license required in order to use Advanced Production Integration (option 2)?

The SAP documentation appears somewhat unclear on this point, as it generally refers to the broader term “production integration” as being included in the basic license, without explicitly distinguishing between the two approaches.

I would really appreciate some clarification on this matter.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Storage Unit Number Regeneration After GI Reversal – Configuration Guidance Needed
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am working in Classic WM / Stock Room Management in SAP S/4HANA and need some guidance regarding Storage Unit (SU) behavior.

Scenario:

  1. When stock from a Storage Unit is fully issued (e.g., GI to cost center – 201), the system deletes the SU number automatically once it becomes empty.

  2. Later, when the Goods Issue is cancelled (reversal), the stock moves back into the warehouse.

  3. However, instead of reusing the original SU number, the system generates a new SU number.

Requirement:

If the GI is cancelled, the system should reuse the same SU number that existed before the issue, rather than creating a new one.

entry.title: Cross Delivery HUs in ASR Scenario with TM
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

It is announced that cross delivery handling units are supported in ASR scenario with the SAP S/4HANA 2025 in SAP. (KBAs: https://me.sap.com/notes/3702034 and https://me.sap.com/notes/3423370) 

Is it only supported with ASR with EWM? or is it also supported with ASR with LWM (lean warehouse management, MM or IM warehouses)? Because I think this part is not clear.

And for both ASR in EWM and LWM, can unified package building (UPB and TM PB) be used to create cross delivery handling units or is it only be created manually? For me this question is matters a lot, because in the case of TU based integration, there are TU documents that consolidates deliveries, which could be considered as consolidation document to create cross delivery HUs. But in ASR such a consolidation document does not exist, if system is not checking FOs. (BTW ASR is not supporting consolidation package building aka late package building in TM.)

Thanks in advance,

Kaya

entry.title: WM Posting Change 309 – Dynamic Bin Instead of TR-ZONE
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

In SAP WM we are using movement type 309 for posting changes.

For movement type 311, we can activate dynamic bin (Scr.) and use requirement type O (Delivery number). The system then determines the outbound delivery number dynamically for the bin.

However, for movement type 309 the system always proposes TR-ZONE for source and destination, and dynamic bin selection is not possible for both at the same time.

Is it possible in standard WM to determine the bin dynamically based on the outbound delivery number for posting change (309)?
Or is an enhancement (user exit/BADI) required?

If anyone has experience with a custom enhancement for this scenario, please share your approach.

entry.title: Error while IBD distribution to EWM in case of STO(Cross Company) if output Type SPED is used.
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I'm trying to use output type SPED against the document type, such as the IBD is automatically created against the GI in the case of STO. But, the IBD is getting stuck in queue in Decentral EWM, with the error as shown in the screenshot below.

I have also observed that this happens only when the system is sharing the HU information in IBD.

Please let me know what needs to be done to rectify it, I checked the SCU I have added all the business attributes.

Regards,
Akhilesh Herkal

entry.title: Unit of Measure for Total Capacity in Storage Bin (LS01N / LS02N / LS03N)
entry.content:
Hi,
Could you please advise what the alternative unit of measure is for the “Total Capacity” field in a storage bin?
In transaction codes LS01N/LS02N/LS03N, I can see the weight unit of measure, but I’m unable to see the unit of measure for Total Capacity. What should the Total Capacity UoM be, or where can it be maintained (Tcode or Table level)?

entry.title: Warehouse Zones for Advanced Interleaving doesn't generate logging
entry.content:
Hi, I’m trying to implement Warehouse Zones for Advanced Interleaving and did set up the following according to the spec

  • Created warehouse zones via SCWM/WZDEF
  • Attached these zones to my bins
  • For each zone, I assigned one storage bin to the corresponding warehouse zone
  • Set up Travel distance Calculation  via /SCWM/WZCAL for my zones
  • And finally activated it for my resource / grp / user using SCWM/WZACT
    • Setup 2 rules just to be sure

  • Futhermore I also set up queue sequence in Interleaving for /SCWM/QTSQ

  • Also activated interleaving via SCWM/QSEC for my Resource Group and via  ‘Cross-Process Settings -> Resource Management -> Define Resource Types’ for my Resource Type

Test my setup: I do get interleaving tasks when testing, but I'm not sure that it works via the my setup of the Warehouse Zone logic as it made some 'funny' choises in assigning WO's

I tried to activate logging via WZ_WHO_DETR via SCWM/ACTLOG, but that doesn't log anything when executing my tasks

My Question(s): Why is my logging not created? Maybe of some missing or incorrect configuration, but I cannot find the rootcause. Any ideas?

Regards Sander.
